data.title: Effect of Intraoperative Superior Laryngeal Nerve Blocks on Patient Outcomes in Microdirect Laryngoscopy for Benign Vocal Fold Lesions: A Randomized Trial
data.brief_summary: Patients undergoing surgery on their vocal cord will either receive a numbing injection to their throat or a saline injection during surgery. Symptoms such as how much coughing or pain patients have after surgery, as well as whether patient's voice gets better will be interrogated.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Patient is aged 18 years or older at time of consent
* Patient has suspected or confirmed benign vocal fold lesion
* Patient is planning to undergo direct laryngoscopy with excision of the vocal fold lesion
* Patient is English or Spanish speaking
* Patient is not pregnant per
data.sponsor: Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey
